output.question: Fetch the birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon
output.findings:
Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and died on February 24, 2001.

His foundational work, particularly his 1948 paper 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication', established the field of information theory. Key contributions include the introduction of the 'bit' as the fundamental unit of information, the development of the source coding theorem, and the formulation of the noisy-channel coding theorem, which defined the maximum rate at which information can be reliably transmitted over a communication channel.

output.final_answer: Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and passed away on February 24, 2001. His three key contributions to information theory include: 1) The introduction of the 'bit' as the fundamental unit of information; 2) The development of the source coding theorem; and 3) The formulation of the noisy-channel coding theorem, which established the maximum rate for reliable information transmission. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon
